intro: Uragoga helferiana, also known as Uragoga, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Uragoga is typically prepared in herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
